name: Persian

formality.description: Persian has a T-V distinction: شما (shomā, formal/plural) vs تو (to, informal/singular). Professional software uses شما-form. Persian also uses elaborate courtesy phrases (تعارف ta'ārof) in formal contexts, which are generally omitted in software UI.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Persian has no grammatical gender. Third-person pronouns (او) are gender-neutral.

formal-shoma.label: شما (Formal)
formal-shoma.description: Standard for professional software and business communication.
formal-shoma.prompt: Formal Persian. Use شما (shomā) for second-person address. Professional register. RTL layout. Modern standard Farsi — avoid excessive تعارف (courtesy phrases). Clear, direct phrasing for software interfaces.

informal-to.label: تو (Informal)
informal-to.description: For consumer apps, social media, and casual contexts.
informal-to.prompt: Informal Persian. Use تو (to) form. Conversational tone for consumer products. RTL layout.

notes: Persian uses Arabic script (modified) but is not Arabic. The script code 'Arab' is correct per ISO 15924.
